ucs.title: [LLM] Nomadic Octopus (DustSquad) Delphi 'Telegram' lure spawning hidden-window PowerShell
ucs.description: Hunts the signature Octopus implant chain: a Delphi-compiled binary masquerading as Telegram/Skype/messenger update (Telegram was banned in Kazakhstan/Uzbekistan, making it Octopus's preferred social-engineering pretext) dropped from a phishing archive, then launching PowerShell with a hidden window to stage second-stage payloads. Targets Central Asian government/diplomatic endpoints — Octopus's exclusive victimology.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","powershell_ise.exe","pwsh.exe") (Processes.process="*-w*hidden*" OR Processes.process="*WindowStyle*Hidden*" OR Processes.process="*-w*1*" OR Processes.process="*-windowstyle*1*") (Processes.parent_process_path="*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*" OR Processes.parent_process_path="*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*" OR Processes.parent_process_path="*\\Downloads\\*" OR Processes.parent_process_path="*\\Users\\Public\\*") (Processes.parent_process_name="*telegram*" OR Processes.parent_process_name="*skype*" OR Processes.parent_process_name="*messenger*" OR Processes.parent_process_name="*browser*update*" OR Processes.parent_process_name="*officeupdate*" OR Processes.parent_process_name="*flash*") by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process_path Processes.process Processes.process_name Processes.process_id Processes.parent_process_id | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | join type=left host parent_process_id [| tstats summariesonly=true values(Filesystem.file_path) as parent_file_path values(Filesystem.file_hash) as parent_hash from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_name="*.exe" by host Filesystem.process_id | rename Filesystem.process_id as parent_process_id]
ucs.kql:
let suspiciousLures = dynamic(["telegram","skype","messenger","browser_update","flashupdate","officeupdate","adobeupdate"]);
let userPaths = dynamic([@"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\Downloads\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\Desktop\"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","powershell_ise.exe")
| where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)(-w(indowstyle)?\s+(hidden|1))|(-nop\b)|(-enc(odedcommand)?\b)|(downloadstring)|(iex\s)|(invoke-expression)"
| where InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (userPaths)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName has_any (suspiciousLures)
   or InitiatingProcessVersionInfoCompanyName has_any ("Embarcadero","Borland","CodeGear")
   or InitiatingProcessVersionInfoOriginalFileName has_any ("Project1.exe","Project2.exe")
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where FileName endswith ".exe" and (FolderPath has_any ("\\Temp\\","\\Downloads\\","\\Roaming\\"))
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName has_any ("winrar.exe","7zG.exe","7zFM.exe","explorer.exe","outlook.exe","WinMail.exe")
    | project DeviceId, DroppedFile=FileName, DropPath=FolderPath, Dropper=InitiatingProcessFileName, DropTime=Timestamp
) on DeviceId
| where DropTime < Timestamp and Timestamp - DropTime < 1h
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath, InitiatingProcessVersionInfoCompanyName, InitiatingProcessVersionInfoOriginalFileName, ProcessCommandLine, Dropper, DroppedFile, DropPath
ucs.rationale: Octopus (the eponymous implant tracked by Kaspersky/ESET as DustSquad's flagship) is Delphi-compiled — a rarity in modern APT tooling that produces distinctive PE metadata (Embarcadero/Borland CompanyName, Project1.exe OriginalFileName). It is reliably delivered as a Telegram/messenger lure to Central Asian targets and immediately spawns PowerShell with `-w hidden` for staging. The combination of

ucs.title: [LLM] Nomadic Octopus second-stage cmd.exe → PowerShell ingress download from Central Asia gov-themed C2
ucs.description: Catches the Octopus post-execution cradle where the Delphi loader uses cmd.exe to invoke PowerShell, which in turn pulls a stage-2 from attacker infrastructure typosquatting Central Asian government domains (e.g. fake Kazakh/Uzbek .gov.kz / .gov.uz lookalikes), a documented DustSquad signature distinguishing it from commodity loaders.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name=cmd.exe Processes.process_name IN (powershell.exe,pwsh.exe) (Processes.process="*DownloadString*" OR Processes.process="*DownloadFile*" OR Processes.process="*Invoke-WebRequest*" OR Processes.process="*WebClient*" OR Processes.process="*BitsTransfer*") by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process_path Processes.process Processes.process_id | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner host process_id [| tstats summariesonly=true values(All_Traffic.dest) as dest values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as dest_port values(All_Traffic.bytes_out) as bytes_out from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.app="powershell.exe" by host All_Traffic.process_id | rename All_Traffic.process_id as process_id] | join type=inner host [| tstats summariesonly=true values(Web.url) as urls values(Web.dest) as web_dest from datamodel=Web.Web where Web.url IN ("*gov-kz*","*gov.kz.*","*-gov.uz*","*mfa-kz*","*kazpost*","*octopus*","*api/c.php*","*/api/d.php*","*/file.php*") by host | fields host urls web_dest] | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql:
let octopusUriPatterns = dynamic(["/api/c.php","/api/d.php","/file.php","/index.php?id=","/get.php?u="]);
let caGovTyposquats = dynamic(["gov-kz","gov.kz.","-gov.uz","mfa-kz","mfa.uz.","kazpost-","egov-kz","-akorda","president-kz"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "cmd.exe"
| where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("DownloadString","DownloadFile","Invoke-WebRequest","iwr ","WebClient","Start-BitsTransfer","FromBase64String")
| extend PSPid = ProcessId
| join kind=inner (
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
    | where RemoteUrl has_any (octopusUriPatterns) or RemoteUrl has_any (caGovTyposquats) or RemoteIPType == "Public"
    | project DeviceId, NetTime=Timestamp, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, RemotePort, PSPid=InitiatingProcessId
) on DeviceId, PSPid
| where NetTime between (Timestamp .. Timestamp + 5m)
| where RemoteUrl has_any (octopusUriPatterns) or RemoteUrl has_any (caGovTyposquats)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, ProcessCommandLine, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, RemotePort
ucs.rationale: DustSquad's Octopus implant uses a documented `/api/c.php` style PHP C2 endpoint, and its operators have repeatedly registered domains impersonating Central Asian government services (Kazakh MFA, e-Gov.kz, Akorda) — a victimology fingerprint no other tracked APT shares at this concentration. Pivoting from a cmd→powershell→download cradle to a network connection hitting either the known Octopus URI
